description: Regression: the 'Select Unit to Fight' dialog listed every Fights First unit TWICE — once under the FIGHTS_FIRST header and again under REMAINING_COMBATS — reading as if those units fought twice. Cause: FightPhase._remaining_units_11e() passed the sequencer's eligible_units(..., only_fights_first=false) straight through, and that list intentionally includes Fights First units (12.04 rules semantics), so the display sections overlapped. NOTE for the helpers: the duplicate button collided on the requested 'Fight_<id>' node name and Godot auto-renamed it to '@Button@N', so the fight_dialog_* helpers match by rendered label too. This drives the real player path: pass both Pile In halves, wait for the FightSelectionDialog, then assert the charged (Fights First) Warboss appears under exactly ONE section header (FIGHTS_FIRST) with exactly ONE button, and the engaged non-FF Custodian Guard appears only under REMAINING_COMBATS. Also guards the dialog's unit labels: buttons resolve through GameState.get_unit_display_name, so the Warboss reads 'Warboss Alpha' (Alpha/Beta disambiguation) and the non-selecting player's Custodian Guard reads its name — not the raw unit id it used to fall back to.
